Name: pac_bayes_latency
Title: PAC-Bayes Frameworks for Latency-Critical Deep Learning Inference with Jacobian-Based Layer Skipping
Short Hypothesis: By integrating PAC-Bayes frameworks with Jacobian-based layer skipping, we can derive strict latency bounds for deep learning models while maintaining accuracy, thus enabling reliable and efficient deployment in latency-critical applications.
Related Work: Existing work on PAC-Bayes frameworks and layer skipping techniques focus on either theoretical foundations or empirical evaluations separately. This proposal uniquely combines these concepts to address the pressing need for latency guarantees in deep learning, distinguishing itself from prior research by its focus on latency-critical inference and the integration of theoretical and empirical methods.
Abstract: Deep learning models are increasingly used in latency-critical applications, necessitating both high accuracy and strict latency guarantees. This proposal introduces a novel approach combining PAC-Bayes frameworks with Jacobian-based layer skipping to achieve reliable and efficient inference. By deriving spectral stability bounds and enforcing strict latency constraints, our method ensures that deep learning models can be deployed with predictable and bounded latency, making them more suitable for real-time applications. Our approach prioritizes empirical validation, mechanistic transparency, and reproducibility, aligning with the need for rigorous and scalable machine learning research.
Experiments: We will conduct experiments on benchmark datasets (e.g., CIFAR-10, ImageNet) using various deep learning architectures (e.g., ResNet, MobileNet). The evaluation metrics will include accuracy, latency, and the trade-off between them. We will compare our approach with state-of-the-art methods for layer skipping and latency reduction, focusing on the achievable latency bounds and the maintenance of model accuracy. Additionally, we will perform ablation studies to understand the contribution of each component of our method to the overall performance.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include the complexity of integrating PAC-Bayes frameworks with layer skipping techniques, which might require significant computational resources and theoretical insights. Another risk is the challenge of balancing accuracy and latency, as overly aggressive layer skipping might compromise model performance. To mitigate these risks, we will iteratively refine our approach based on empirical results and theoretical analysis, ensuring that our method is both effective and efficient.
